gemma4_qknorm.note: Marginal improvement; the bandit only allocated 4 of the 12 configs to this bucket on this single 12-config run.

gemma4_qknorm_global.note: Slightly worse than the previous run because m64_n64_w4_s2 was not re-sampled this run; it remains the historical winner in the database.

notes: Zero runtime failures recorded across 148 (config, shape) pairs. The shmem-filter removal did not crash any configs on this run, so the failure-as-reward-zero path was untested in practice — but the system is now ready to learn from any future failures.

Only 4 of 12 configs were applied to each qknorm bucket because the bandit prioritises Thompson-sampled configs from already-warm buckets and the qknorm cells share the global pool of 12 slots. To stress-test feasibility learning, a longer multi-iteration run (or a larger configs-per-run budget) is needed.
The previous best m64_n64_w4_s2 is still in the historical database; this run did not re-sample it. The reported delta is single-run vs single-run, not cumulative-best vs cumulative-best.
